items.title: Annotate shapes at every layer boundary
items.avoid: Writing model code without noting tensor shapes between layers, then debugging with random transposes.
items.prefer: Comment or assert shapes after each layer: X (B x d_in), W (d_in x d_out), output (B x d_out).
items.why: Shape mismatches are the most common dense-layer bug; annotations connect code to the matrix-multiplication view.
items.example:
```python
x = batch.view(batch.size(0), -1)  # B x 784
h = self.fc1(x)                    # B x 256
assert h.shape == (x.size(0), 256)
```

items.title: Match output layer width to the task
items.avoid: Using a single output neuron for multi-class classification or ten outputs for binary classification without a clear mapping.
items.prefer: Binary: 1 output (with sigmoid) or 2 logits; K-class: K output neurons (with softmax over K).
items.why: Output width defines the prediction vector; wrong width breaks loss functions and metric interpretation.

items.title: Separate depth from activation modules
items.avoid: Counting ReLU, Dropout, or BatchNorm as extra "layers" when reporting network depth.
items.prefer: Count hidden Linear (or Conv) transformation layers for depth; treat activations and normalization as operators on a layer's activations.
items.why: Consistent depth reporting aligns with literature and tools like torchinfo that list transformation layers separately from activations.
items.example: `Linear → ReLU → Linear → ReLU → Linear` has depth 2 (two hidden Linear layers), not depth 4.
